cycle_life.label: Cycle Life Test
data_acquisition: Use a logging cadence that preserves the ageing profile, reference checks, and temperature excursions.
Store reference performance test outputs against equivalent cycle count or Ah throughput.
Keep breakpoint metadata explicit so each RPT can be linked back to the preceding ageing block.
planner_notes: The white paper defines a DOE-style framework rather than one single executable protocol. The plan must be finalized per project.
Keep the RPT stack short enough that the check-up itself does not dominate degradation.

campaign_framework.summary: Keep the handbook cycle-life flow as the execution spine, then use DOE literature to narrow hard-to-change factors and use breakpoint diagnostics only where they add real value.
recommended_sequence: Lock the application-relevant stressor family before varying fast knobs.
Run baseline reference tests before the first ageing block.
Age in repeated blocks and stop only at explicit checkpoints for RPTs.
Escalate to impedance or slow-curve diagnostics only at selected breakpoints or end of life.
campaign_framework.replicate_guidance: Use at least two cells per condition when the campaign is comparative, and tighten start-of-life matching if pack or parallel-string effects matter.
default_breakpoint_examples: Every 50-125 cycles or equivalent throughput block
Every 14-28 days when time-based interruption is more practical
EIS or slow-curve diagnostics at beginning, selected middle checkpoints, and end of life
stop_criteria_options: Capacity or energy SOH at or below 80%
Maximum cycle count or elapsed duration reached

reference_check_policy.summary: Baseline and periodic RPTs remain mandatory. Keep the core reference set stable, and add heavier diagnostics only at selected breakpoints where mechanism attribution justifies the interruption cost.

rpt_cadence_mode.selection_note: Use a stable core RPT bundle at every checkpoint. Reserve impedance and slow-curve extensions for selected breakpoints or end of life.

reference_temperature_c.restart_requirement: Return to the reference temperature before each RPT and wait for thermal equilibrium before the first check-up measurement.

default_breakpoint_examples: Baseline before the first ageing block
Repeat the core RPT every 50-125 cycles or equivalent throughput block
Reserve impedance and slow-curve extensions for selected checkpoints and end of life

core_rpt_set.label: Cycle-life core RPT set
core_rpt_set.summary: Track the minimum ageing observables that stay stable over the full campaign by using the same reference stack at baseline, intermediate checkpoints, and end of life.

step_bundle.details: Return the cell to the declared RPT reference temperature and wait for thermal equilibrium before the first measurement step.
Run the standard cycle reference block to reset the comparison state before the capacity and pulse checks.
Run the reference capacity test at the declared check-up condition.
Run the pulse / HPPC block to capture resistance and power-capability drift.

checkpoint_extension_tests.label: Impedance extension
checkpoint_extension_tests.summary: Add frequency-resolved resistance information at selected checkpoints when mechanism attribution matters.

step_bundle.details: Run the impedance block only at selected checkpoints or end of life after the core RPT bundle is complete.

checkpoint_extension_tests.label: Slow-curve extension
checkpoint_extension_tests.summary: Capture OCV- and ICA-ready traces only when additional degradation-mode evidence is needed.

step_bundle.details: Run the slow-curve extension only at selected checkpoints when OCV or ICA evidence is needed for attribution.
Run the full core RPT bundle before any ageing block begins.

checkpoint_templates.label: Intermediate cycle-life checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger: At the declared cycle, throughput, or elapsed-time breakpoint

step_bundle.details: Pause the ageing loop only at the declared breakpoint and capture the preceding block metadata.
Run the locked core RPT bundle first at every intermediate checkpoint.
Add the impedance extension only at selected breakpoints or when the review plan explicitly calls for it.
Add the slow-curve extension only when extra degradation-mode evidence is needed.
Resume the ageing loop only after the checkpoint results are reviewed against the continue or stop criteria.

checkpoint_templates.label: End-of-life cycle-life checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger: When the stop criterion is reached

step_bundle.details: Run the core RPT bundle one final time at the declared end-of-life threshold.
Add the impedance extension to strengthen end-of-life attribution.
Add the slow-curve extension if OCV or ICA evidence is part of the reviewed end-of-life package.
Close the campaign with the reviewed end-of-life report, stop reason, and diagnostic bundle summary.
